picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Carolina Hurricanes
picks.rationale: All four games in this Stanley Cup Final series have gone over the total, with a remarkable average of 8.2 combined goals per game. The total line has already moved from an opening of 6 to the current 6.5, indicating sharp money on the over. Both teams possess skilled offenses and are generating significant shot volume, suggesting a continuation of this high-scoring trend. Carolina's expected starting goaltender, Brandon Bussi, while performing well in Game 4, is still relatively unproven in this high-pressure environment, and Vegas's Carter Hart has allowed at least four goals in every game of the series.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Carolina Hurricanes
picks.rationale: The Carolina Hurricanes hold a significant situational advantage playing at home in this pivotal Game 5, with the series tied 2-2. Carolina has an impressive record of winning nine of their last ten home games following a road win, demonstrating their ability to bounce back effectively. Conversely, the Vegas Golden Knights have struggled on the road after a home loss, with a 1-7 record in their last eight such games. Furthermore, Carolina is expected to start Brandon Bussi in net, who performed well in Game 4, while Vegas's Carter Hart has been consistently allowing four or more goals per game in the series.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Carolina Hurricanes
picks.rationale: Given the Carolina Hurricanes' strong home performance, especially after a road win (9-1 in their last 10), and the struggles of Vegas Golden Knights' goaltender Carter Hart, a multi-goal victory for Carolina is a strong possibility. The Hurricanes have been averaging 4.3 goals per game in this series, and their offense is clicking. With the series tied 2-2, Carolina will be looking to capitalize on home-ice advantage and Hart's recent form, who has allowed at least four goals in each of the first four games. The attractive odds for the -1.5 spread offer excellent value considering these factors.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Carolina Hurricanes
picks.rationale: Carolina Hurricanes forward Jordan Staal is in exceptional form, having scored five goals through the first four games of the Stanley Cup Final, finding the net in every game of the series. Staal is confirmed active and is expected to play a key role in Game 5. His consistent scoring in high-stakes situations makes him a prime candidate to score again. He is a veteran presence who thrives in front of the net, where he has done most of his damage in this series.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Carolina Hurricanes
picks.rationale: Brett Howden has been a significant offensive contributor for the Vegas Golden Knights in the playoffs, leading the team with 14 postseason goals. Crucially, Howden has scored in both of the previous games played in Carolina during this series. He is confirmed active and is expected to be a key offensive threat for Vegas. His ability to find the back of the net, particularly on the road against the Hurricanes, makes this a high-confidence pick.

picks.game: St. Louis Cardinals vs New York Mets
picks.rationale: The New York Mets are severely hampered by injuries to key players like Francisco Lindor, Jorge Polanco, Kodai Senga, and Luis Robert Jr., significantly weakening their lineup and pitching staff. In contrast, the St. Louis Cardinals are on a strong six-game winning streak, demonstrating excellent recent form. The Mets' offense is also ranked among the worst in baseball, further diminishing their chances against a surging Cardinals team. The Cardinals also boast a strong 39-26 ATS record, while the Mets are 6-17 ATS as home favorites, indicating a historical struggle to cover at home.

The Colorado Rockies have already swept the first two games of this series against the Chicago Cubs, showing strong home performance. The Cubs are in a significant slump, with a 7-22 record recently and averaging only 3.2 runs per game during this period. Furthermore, Cubs starting pitcher Edward Cabrera has a high home run rate, which is particularly dangerous at hitter-friendly Coors Field. Despite public money heavily favoring the Cubs, sharp money has moved towards the Rockies, indicating professional bettors see value in the home underdog.

picks.game: Atlanta Braves vs Chicago White Sox
picks.rationale: The Chicago White Sox enter this game with significant momentum, having won the first two games of this series against the Atlanta Braves and boasting a 10-5 record in their last 15 games, including seven straight home wins. The Braves are without star outfielder Ronald Acuña Jr., who is on the 10-day IL with his second hamstring injury of the season, a major blow to their potent offense. While Braves pitcher Martin Perez has a better ERA, the White Sox's recent performance and home advantage, coupled with Acuña's absence, make them a strong value pick at near pick'em odds.

picks.game: Los Angeles Dodgers vs Pittsburgh Pirates
picks.rationale: The Pittsburgh Pirates are coming off an impressive comeback victory against the Los Angeles Dodgers on Wednesday, highlighted by Tyler Callihan's two home runs, suggesting a surge in team morale and offensive capability. The Dodgers will be without key catcher Will Smith, who is day-to-day with a neck issue and missed Wednesday's game, potentially impacting their offensive production and game calling. The Pirates have been playing compelling baseball recently, and getting 1.5 runs at home against a Dodgers team missing a crucial player offers good value.

The Baltimore Orioles secured a decisive 7-2 victory over the Seattle Mariners in their previous game, demonstrating strong recent form at home. The Mariners are dealing with several key injuries, including J.P. Crawford (hand) and Cal Raleigh (oblique), which could hinder their offensive output. While the Orioles also have injuries, their recent win and the home-field advantage at even money present a favorable betting opportunity against a Mariners team that might be struggling with lineup depth.

Mexico has demonstrated strong defensive capabilities recently, securing six clean sheets in their last eight matches across all competitions. South Africa's offensive output has been limited, as they are winless in their last five games and have averaged only 0.8 goals per match during that period. World Cup opening matches often tend to be cautious and low-scoring affairs, with teams prioritizing defensive solidity due to the high stakes and early tournament nerves. The historical head-to-head record between these two nations shows an average of 2.5 goals per game, with their only World Cup encounter in 2010 ending in a 1-1 draw.
